meta.title: Outpatient Addiction Therapy This Week | PhD/PsyD | Discreet | WTG
meta.description: Outpatient addiction therapy with a doctoral therapist this week. PhD/PsyD only, cash-pay discretion, $280-300/session. Not residential. Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, online.

hero.h1: Outpatient addiction therapy this week, with a PhD or PsyD therapist.
hero.subhead: Whether you're working through alcohol use, recreational substance challenges, prescription dependence, or maintenance after primary treatment, your first session can be this week. Outpatient, doctoral-level, cash-pay private practice. Not residential rehab. Your story stays between you and your team.
hero.trustLine: ✓ First session this week ✓ $280 to $300 per session ✓ Cash-pay discretion, OON claim filed if you choose

treat.h2: Outpatient addiction therapy is what we do, and the rest of the team is here when the work asks for more
treat.subhead: Six ways our doctoral team supports adults working through substance use and the mental health work that goes alongside it. Every clinician PhD or PsyD.

cards.title: Outpatient substance use therapy
cards.body: Doctoral support for working through alcohol use, recreational substance challenges, and prescription dependence in an outpatient private-practice setting.

cards.title: Co-occurring mental health
cards.body: Anxiety, depression, trauma, and other conditions that often go alongside substance use. Treated together, on the same team, not in two separate appointments.

cards.title: Maintenance and relapse prevention
cards.body: Doctoral support after primary treatment, when the work is staying on the path you've already started.

cards.title: Family and couples affected by substance use
cards.body: Doctoral therapists for partners, kids, and families navigating a loved one's substance use.

cards.title: Psychiatry on the same team
cards.body: Board-certified psychiatrists when medication is part of your treatment plan. One care plan, not two.

cards.title: Honest about what we don't do
cards.body: We are not a residential rehab, detox center, or 24-hour monitoring program. If that's what you need, your coordinator will refer you to a partner who is.

comparison.h2: How outpatient addiction therapy at WTG compares to the rest of the field
comparison.subhead: What you'll get from most options versus what happens at WTG.

rows.them: A facility-based program that puts your situation on file with your insurance and shows up in your medical record.
rows.us: A private-practice outpatient setting with cash-pay options. Your story stays between you and your team unless you choose to submit a superbill.

rows.them: LCDCs or peer counselors as the primary provider, with no doctoral oversight.
rows.us: Doctoral therapists (PhD or PsyD) trained to treat substance use alongside the underlying anxiety, depression, or trauma.

rows.them: Single-track 12-step or single-modality framework regardless of fit.
rows.us: Evidence-based work matched to your situation, integrating cognitive behavioral therapy, motivational interviewing, and other approaches.

rows.them: Six-week waitlist before your first session.
rows.us: Most patients have their first session the same week they call, match confirmed within 24 hours.

rows.them: Out-of-network paperwork dumped on you to figure out alone if you choose to use insurance.
rows.us: Reimbursify files your OON claim for you if you opt in, and most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.

howItWorks.h2: Four steps from your first call to your first session
howItWorks.subhead: Under five minutes to start. No long forms, no chatbots, a real person answers and asks three questions about where you are in this work.

steps.title: Call or tap to book
steps.body: Reach our patient coordinator by phone or online. A real person gets back to you the same day, on a five-minute call.

steps.title: Get matched in 24 hours
steps.body: We pair you with a doctoral therapist for what you're working through, your schedule, and how you like to work. Match confirmed within 24 hours.

steps.title: First session
steps.body: Meet in person or over secure video, often within the same week.

steps.title: Make progress
steps.body: Concrete tools and check-ins so you can see the change in your day-to-day, not just in session.

whyWTG.h2: Five reasons high-functioning adults choose WTG for outpatient substance use work

bullets.title: Private-practice discretion
bullets.body: Outpatient setting, cash-pay options. No facility intake forms. No insurance disclosures unless you choose to submit a superbill. Your work stays between you and your team.

bullets.title: Doctoral, substance-use-experienced therapists
bullets.body: PhD and PsyD only, with training in substance use work and co-occurring mental health. Not LCDC-only, not peer-counselor only.

bullets.title: Substance use AND the mental health that goes alongside it
bullets.body: Most facilities treat one or the other. Our doctoral team treats both, with the same care plan.

bullets.title: Real coordinator match in 24 hours
bullets.body: A person on our care team listens to where you are in this work and picks the therapist most likely to fit. Match confirmed within 24 hours.

bullets.title: In person or online, your call
bullets.body: Brooklyn, Austin, Miami offices, plus secure video across NY, TX, and FL. Most new patients start the same week.

clinicians.eyebrow: Meet a few of our therapists
clinicians.h2: Doctoral substance use specialists across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online
clinicians.subhead: Our coordinator can pair you with a doctoral therapist whose practice area includes substance use work. The four below are placeholder slots for real WTG specialists; final names and headshots replace these before launch.

midCTA.body: Same-week first sessions for outpatient substance use therapy across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online. The hardest part is the first call.

pricing.subhead: We're a cash-pay practice. No insurer dictating how many sessions you get, no diagnosis code filed to your permanent medical record.
pricing.reimbursement: Many patients have out-of-network benefits. We partner with Reimbursify, an independent service that submits your out-of-network claim to your insurer for you. Most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.
steps: We charge you the session fee at the time of service.
We send you a superbill you can submit to your insurance.
Reimbursify files the claim for you. You get reimbursed directly.

faq.h2: Everything adults ask before the first session

items.q: Will my insurance company or employer find out?
items.a: When you pay cash for outpatient therapy, your sessions do not appear on your insurance record or in any employer-visible system. If you choose to submit an OON superbill for reimbursement, your insurance will see a diagnosis code on the claim. Many of our patients choose cash-pay specifically to keep this work entirely between them and their care team. Your coordinator can walk you through what each option means before you decide.

items.q: How much does therapy cost, and how long will it take?
items.a: Sessions are $280 to $300 each. Most patients meet weekly to start, often shifting to every other week as the work progresses. Patients doing focused work on substance use plus co-occurring anxiety, depression, or trauma often see meaningful change in 16 to 24 sessions. Patients in maintenance after primary treatment often stay longer at lower frequency. Your therapist will set goals with you in the first sessions and check progress regularly. We're not in the business of keeping you in therapy forever.

items.q: Are you a residential rehab or a detox center?
items.a: No. We're an outpatient, doctoral-level private practice. We work with adults who are not in active medical detox and who can safely engage in weekly or twice-weekly therapy sessions. If you need residential treatment, medical detox, or 24-hour care, your coordinator will help you find a partner provider that fits.

items.q: Do you prescribe medication for substance use treatment?
items.a: Our psychiatrists prescribe medications that support outpatient substance use treatment when it's clinically appropriate. Whether a medication-supported track is the right fit depends on your specific situation. Your coordinator can match you with a psychiatrist who has experience with this work if that's part of what you're looking for.

items.q: Do you take my insurance for outpatient addiction therapy?
items.a: We're a cash-pay practice, so we don't bill insurance directly. Many patients pay cash to keep their substance use work entirely off their insurance record. If you'd rather submit a superbill for out-of-network reimbursement, your insurance will see a diagnosis code on the claim. Most plans reimburse 50 to 80 percent of the session fee, and we partner with Reimbursify to file the claim for you.

form.eyebrow: Ready to take the next step?
form.h2: Tell us a little about what you're looking for
form.subhead: Our patient coordinator will call or email you back the same day. Discreet, no pressure, no waitlists. The first session can be this week.
form.consent: By leaving your phone or email, you are consenting to receiving communications from us via text or email and that may include communications about your appointments, services, and marketing information related to our services. You may opt out of marketing information at any time. Your contact information will never be sold to any third party and will only be shared to the extent necessary to perform any services on your behalf.
form.phoneFallback: or call 347-970-6757 to speak with our coordinator now.
form.reassurance: Same-day response. No waitlists, no runaround.
